Article.ArticleTitle: Safe AI-enabled digital health technologies need built-in open feedback.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Else Kröner Fresenius Center for Digital Health, TUD Dresden University of Technology, Dresden, Germany.
Nuffield Department of Surgical Sciences, University of Oxford, Oxford, UK.
Division of Thoracic and Endocrine Surgery, Geneva University Hospital, Geneva, Switzerland.
National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE), London, UK.
Department of Neurology, Heidelberg University Hospital, Heidelberg, Germany.
School of Medicine, Trinity College Dublin, Dublin, Ireland.
Else Kröner Fresenius Center for Digital Health, TUD Dresden University of Technology, Dresden, Germany. stephen.gilbert@tu-dresden.de.

Grant.Agency: EC | Horizon 2020 Framework Programme (EU Framework Programme for Research and Innovation H2020)

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Competing interests: S.G. declares a nonfinancial interest as an advisory group member of the Ernst & Young-coordinated ‘Study on regulatory governance and innovation in the field of medical devices’, conducted on behalf of the Directorate-General for Health and Food Safety (SANTE) of the European Commission. S.G. also declares consulting relationships with Una Health, Lindus Health, Flo, ICURA, Rock Health, Thymia, FORUM Institut für Management, High-Tech Gründerfonds Management, the Directorate-General for Research and Innovation of the European Commission, and Ada Health. S.G. furthermore declares share options in Ada Health. A.C. is an employee of the National Institute of Health and Care Excellence (NICE), UK. The views expressed in this article are those of author and not the views of NICE. L.R. declares shares in Shift Medical. T.M. has engaged in paid clinical training for the National Standards Authority of Ireland; is a previous co-chair of the Clinical Investigation and Evaluation Working Group; and is a member of the medical device expert panel of the European Commission. T.M. was also an unpaid advisory board member for Pumpinheart.

Reference.Citation: Alowais, S. A. et al. BMC Med. Educ. 23, 689 (2023).
Wu, C. et al. BMJ Open 13, e066322 (2023).
Welzel, C. et al. J. Med. Internet Res. 25, e50158 (2023).
Gilbert, S., Pimenta, A., Stratton-Powell, A., Welzel, C. & Melvin, T. Mayo Clin. Proc. Digit. Health 1, 276–287 (2023).
Fornasier, G., Francescon, S., Leone, R. & Baldo, P. Int. J. Clin. Pharm. 40, 744–747 (2018).
Higson, G. R. Medical Device Safety: the Regulation of Medical Devices for Public Health and Safety (Routledge & CRC, 2001).
El Fassi, S. C. et al. Nat. Med. 30, 2718–2720 (2024).
Gagliardi, A. R. et al. BMJ Qual. Saf. 27, 190–198 (2018).
Wong, A. et al. JAMA Intern. Med. 181, 1065–1070 (2021).
Tase, A., Ni, M. Z., Buckle, P. W. & Hanna, G. B. BMJ Open Qual. 11, e001849 (2022).
Welzel, C., Brückner, S., Brightwell, C., Fenech, M. & Gilbert, S. PLOS Digit. Health 3, e0000656 (2024).
